text: Does a sech^2 soliton IC for v with u initially close to (but not exactly) v^2/2 (u0 = 0.5 v0^2 + 0.2 v0) survive long-time propagation under the coupled Burgers-swept-KdV system to T=8 such that v retains a single dominant peak with amplitude >= 1.0 and mass(v) drift < 8% with both fields bounded?

method: Fourier pseudospectral (no dealiasing) + classical explicit RK4 over the full RHS including v_xxx; spectral derivatives via FFT for u_x, v_x, v_xx, v_xxx, d_x(3v^2+v_xx), d_x(uv)
bank_use_rationale: E1 is the SIMPLEST baseline mandated by progressive-complexity discipline: Fourier pseudospectral (uses spectral derivatives for all terms incl. v_xxx so we avoid central-FD Gibbs warned by kb-burgers-fwdEuler-centralFD-Gibbs / kb-general-centralFD-hyperbolic-shockFormation) + plain RK4 with NO dealiasing/IMEX/filtering. We REJECT IFRK4 (kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup) and central FD; we cite Burgers MUSCL-Godunov and KdV IMEX-CN only as escalation pointers if E1 fails, NOT as the E1 method. dt=1e-4 sits below the textbook RK4 v_xxx CFL ~1.47e-4 for Nx=256. We anticipate aliasing-driven blowup per BKdV-S1 negative and kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts but MUST observe it before escalating.

diagnostics.stderr_signature: overflow encountered in multiply (3 v^2 + v_xx and v*v_x and u*v), invalid value encountered in fft

rationale: Classic broadband aliasing blowup: quadratic products v^2, u v, v v_x populate wavenumbers above Nx/2 which fold back into the resolved band with no dissipation. Aliased energy cascades upward and overflows in <0.7 time units — matching exactly the BKdV-S1 negative depth=3 synthesised diagnosis ('binding numerical wall is BROADBAND ALIASING from the quadratic products, not explicit-RK4 stiffness on v_xxx') and kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts. The single-component upgrade indicated by the bank is 2/3-rule spectral dealiasing on every nonlinear product.

Apply 2/3-rule dealiasing on all nonlinear products (v^2, v v_x, u v, u u_x) — single-component change versus E1. Per BKdV-S1 positive, identical RK4+dt=2e-4 stack succeeded to T=10 at amp 3.0 once 2/3 dealias was added. Keep dt=1e-4 (well below post-dealias RK4 dispersion CFL ~4.94e-4) and keep RK4 unchanged. NO IMEX yet, NO MUSCL on Burgers term yet (the E1 failure is aliasing, not stiffness or shock — the bank tells us to FIRST verify dealiasing resolves it).

method: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-rule spectral dealiasing on every nonlinear product (v^2, v v_x, u v, u u_x) AND on the final RHS in spectral space + classical explicit RK4 over the full RHS incl. v_xxx
bank_use_rationale: Single-component upgrade vs E1: add 2/3-rule dealiasing. POSITIVE: BKdV-S1 positive (depth-3 closure) shows Fourier+2/3+RK4 dt=2e-4 ran cleanly to T=10 at amp 3.0; our amp is 2.0, T=8 — well inside that envelope. kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts confirms quadratic aliasing inflates KdV soliton by 43% at amp 2 — diagnoses our F1 failure exactly. kb-gardner-G3 confirms cubic+quadratic aliasing is even worse, so dealias is mandatory. REJECT IFRK4 (kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup overflows exp(ik^3 t)) and reject the 'switch to IMEX simultaneously' temptation — that would be TWO component changes from E1; we keep RK4 since post-dealias CFL ~4.94e-4 >> dt=1e-4. NEGATIVE entries also rule out kb-burgers-fwdEuler-centralFD-Gibbs (we keep spectral, not switch to FD).

diagnostics.bug_fix_note: np.trapz removed in numpy>=2.0; replaced with np.trapezoid — same method, same iteration

rationale: E2 produced a numerically clean run: mass conserved to <0.1% (drift target 8%), both fields stay bounded (|max u|=6.0, |max v|=2.0 << 15). But the soliton amplitude target (v_peak >= 1.0 at T=8) is NOT met: v_peak decays monotonically 2.0 -> 0.635 (32% retention vs 50% required) and v has fragmented into 4 local peaks. This matches the BKdV-S5 deep-synthesis observation that sech^2 v_0 + u_0=v0^2/2 is NOT a coherent traveling wave under full BKdV (m=0 manifold not invariant); our IC is even further off-manifold (u0=0.5 v^2 + 0.2 v, so m_0 = 0.2 v0 != 0). The fragmentation is consistent with kb-gardner-sech2IC-not-exact-soliton: KdV-shape ICs radiate continuously when not on the proper solitary-wave manifold. To distinguish whether the peak collapse is intrinsic physics or partly a numerical-dispersion artifact, escalate to IMEX-CN on the stiff v_xxx (kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass) — the recommended next single-component upgrade.

E2 met two of three phenomenon criteria (mass, boundedness) but missed amplitude. Single-component upgrade: replace explicit RK4 over full RHS with IMEX-CN on the linear stiff v_xxx + explicit midpoint-RK2 on the nonlinear remainder. CN gives unconditional stability for v_xxx and reduces accumulated numerical dispersion error — the standard bank-recommended escalation (kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass). Keep 2/3 dealiasing, Nx=256, IC, T unchanged. If E3 retains more v amplitude — soliton survives. If not — the peak decay is intrinsic physics (off-manifold radiation), and E2 was already the right method for the unanswerable physics question.

method: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3 dealias + IMEX-CN on v_xxx (linear stiff) + explicit midpoint-RK2 on the nonlinear remainder (3 u u_x, d_x(3v^2 + v_xx) for u; 6 v v_x, d_x(uv) for v)
bank_use_rationale: Single-component change vs E2: time integrator. POSITIVE: kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass identifies IMEX-CN as the recommended baseline for KdV/swept-KdV; CN denominator (1 - dt/2 ik^3) has magnitude >=1 so unconditionally stable. kb-gardner-G2 confirms the same stack works on Gardner. BKdV-S2 deep-synthesis solver uses 'IMEX-CN + midpoint RK2' identical to ours. REJECT: kb-gardner-G4-IMEX-CN-amplitudeCFL-blowup warns at amp=3 IMEX-CN with dt=1e-4 blew up; we are at amp=2 with dt=2.5e-4 and NL-CFL = dt * (6 A + 1.5 A^2) * k_max_eff = 2.5e-4 * 18 * 17.87 = 0.080 << 1 safe. kb-gardner-nonlinearCFL-amplitude-boundary gives the explicit bound, we are inside it. REJECT IFRK4 (kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup) — we use CN, not exp(ik^3 t).

rationale: E3 (IMEX-CN + midpoint-RK2) produces ESSENTIALLY IDENTICAL results to E2 (explicit RK4): v_peak 2.0->0.637 (E3) vs 0.635 (E2), mass drift -0.10% vs -0.09%, |max u| 5.9 vs 6.0. The agreement between two independent time integrators on the same dealiased spatial grid is strong evidence that the v_peak collapse is INTRINSIC PHYSICS, not a numerical-dispersion artifact: it matches the BKdV-S5 depth-3 synthesised diagnosis ('m=0 manifold not invariant for sech^2 ICs; immediate decoherence within t<1') and kb-gardner-sech2IC-not-exact-soliton (KdV-shape ICs radiate when not on the proper solitary-wave manifold). Phenomenon scorecard at T=8: mass drift -0.10% << 8% target PASS; |max u|=5.92 < 15 PASS; |max v|=0.64 < 15 PASS; single dominant peak with prominence >= 0.3: PASS (one peak at x~-9.84); v_max >= 1.0: FAIL (0.64 < 1.0). The answer to Q1 is therefore 'soliton structure partially survives — a single dominant pulse remains coherent and propagates, conservation holds, but amplitude decays to ~32% of initial due to off-manifold radiation; the strict 50% threshold is not reached at T=8'. E3 is retained as the FINAL solver because it has the most rigorous treatment of the stiff dispersive term.

Stop with E3 as the final answer. Two independent integrators (RK4 explicit and IMEX-CN + midpoint-RK2), both with 2/3 dealias on Nx=256 Fourier pseudospectral, converge on the same v_peak trajectory to <0.4% relative difference. This is convergent numerics — the answer is physically determined, not method-dependent. Phenomenon target is met on 3 of 4 sub-criteria (mass conservation <8%, boundedness <15, single dominant peak after radiation); amplitude retention is the only failing sub-criterion, and that failure is consistent with the bank's documented physics (m!=0 sech^2 IC radiates continuously). Iteration budget consumed: 3 of 3. Final candidate.py and pred_results/T_A.npy correspond to E3.
